question.text: The primary advantage of a Systematic Investment Plan (SIP) in mutual funds is:

answer.text: Guaranteed returns over the investment period
Tax exemption on all SIP investments
Rupee cost averaging — buying more units when prices are low and fewer when high
Zero exit load on all SIP redemptions

explanation.text: **The Logic:** In an SIP, the same amount is invested regularly regardless of market levels. When NAV is low, more units are purchased; when NAV is high, fewer units are purchased. Over time, this averages out the cost of acquisition — called rupee cost averaging. SIPs do NOT guarantee returns. **The Trap:** SIPs don't provide tax exemption (only ELSS SIPs get 80C benefit) or guaranteed returns — they provide discipline and averaging benefits. **Cross-Exam Context:** SIP and rupee cost averaging are tested in V-A, V-B, and X-A. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** SIPs work best in volatile markets where the averaging effect is most pronounced — during prolonged bull markets, lump sum may outperform SIP.
